original: Date returned to in "Back to the Future" by Marty McFly

Iceberg twice size of Rhode Island sighted in Antarctic
Space probe Ulyssus completes 1st passage behind the Sun

George Foreman(45) KOsMichael Moorerto win boxing Heavyweight championship
ATS-3 launched by US to take first pictures of full Earth disc

NBA's LA Lakers starts a 33 game consecutive victory streak
Montreal Canadien centerJean Béliveauscores 3 goals in 44 seconds on future Hall of Fame goaltenderTerry Sawchukin 4-2 win v Boston Bruins; 2nd fastest NHL hat trick

American chess grandmasterBobby Fischerdefeats Russian Boris Spassky in an unofficial match in Belgrade dubbed the "Revenge Match of the 20th Century"

Jim Tabor, American MLB 3rd baseman (2 x grand slams in one game), born in New Hope, Alabama (d. 1953)

First US automobile patent granted, to George B. Selden for a gasoline-driven car

Japan beats MLB all stars 2-1 in Tokyo (Game 1 of 7)
Johnny Damon, American baseball outfielder (World Series 2004 Boston Red Sox, 2009 NY Yankees; MLB All-Star 2002, 05), born in Fort Riley, Kansas
Alexei Yashin, Russian ice hockey center (Team Russia Olympic silver 1998, bronze 2002; Ottawa Senators; IIHF Hall of Fame), born in Sverdlovsk, Russia
Abédi Pelé, Ghanaian footballer (captain of Ghana National Team), born in Kibi, Eastern Region, Ghana

NCAA passing record set at 571 yards (Marc Wilson, Brigham Young)

Armin Shimerman, American actor (Principal Snyder-Buffy the Vampire Slayer), born in Lakewood, New Jersey
Uwe Seeler, German soccer striker (72 caps West Germany; Hamburger SV 476 games, 404 goals), born in Hamburg, Germany (d. 2022)

NFL St Louis Cardinals' Bill Stacy, returns 2 interceptions for TDs vs Dallas Cowboys
Cleveland Cavaliers lose 24th consecutive game (NBA record)
